description: International outlaw motorcycle club founded in McCook, Illinois in 1935 by John Davis. With over 5,000 members and 441 chapters in 43 countries, it is the second-largest outlaw biker club worldwide. Designated an organized crime syndicate by U.S., Canadian, and European agencies. Members typically ride Harley-Davidson motorcycles.

country_links.context: The Outlaws expanded into Australia by establishing a chapter there.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 1977, the Outlaws became an international club when several chapters of the Satan's Choice Motorcycle Club in Canada "patched over".
country_links.context: The Outlaws expanded into Canada by patching over several chapters of the Satan's Choice Motorcycle Club.

country_links.evidence_quote: The club further expanded internationally, into France in 1993...
country_links.context: The Outlaws expanded into France by establishing a chapter there.

country_links.evidence_quote: In November 2006, the Outlaws became the first major outlaw motorcycle club to open a chapter in the Far East when a full charter was awarded to a club in Okinawa City .
country_links.context: The Outlaws opened a chapter in Okinawa City, Japan.
The Outlaws expanded into Norway by establishing a chapter there.
